title: Multi-Agent GraphRAG: A Text-to-Cypher Framework for Labeled Property Graphs
topics: GraphRAG; multi-agent; text-to-Cypher; labeled property graph; Memgraph
summary: A modular agentic GraphRAG system that turns natural language into Cypher queries over labeled property graphs. It uses iterative correction and feedback to improve generated graph queries, with demonstrations on CypherBench and an IFC building digital twin.

title: Towards Open-World Retrieval-Augmented Generation on Knowledge Graph: A Multi-Agent Collaboration Framework
topics: AnchorRAG; knowledge graph; open-world retrieval; multi-agent; multi-hop
summary: Introduces AnchorRAG, where agents infer possible anchor entities and explore knowledge graph paths in parallel. The design improves robustness when entity linking is uncertain or ambiguous.

title: MAO-ARAG: Multi-Agent Orchestration for Adaptive Retrieval-Augmented Generation
topics: adaptive RAG; multi-agent orchestration; planner; reinforcement learning; cost control
summary: Uses a planner agent to dynamically select query reformulation, document selection, and generation agents for each query. Reinforcement learning balances answer quality against latency and cost.

title: GraphRAG-R1: Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Process-Constrained Reinforcement Learning
topics: GraphRAG; reinforcement learning; multi-hop reasoning; hybrid graph-text retrieval; cost-aware rewards
summary: Trains GraphRAG workflows to retrieve and reason adaptively instead of following fixed heuristics. Process rewards encourage useful retrieval while penalizing shallow retrieval and excessive reasoning cost.

title: CIIR@LiveRAG 2025: Optimizing Multi-Agent Retrieval Augmented Generation through Self-Training
topics: multi-agent RAG; self-training; planning; searching; reasoning; coordination
summary: Presents mRAG, a specialized-agent framework optimized with reward-guided trajectory sampling. It was evaluated in the SIGIR 2025 LiveRAG competition setting for complex real-world retrieval tasks.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Comprehensive Survey of Architectures, Enhancements, and Robustness Frontiers
topics: RAG survey; architecture taxonomy; robustness; evaluation; hybrid retrieval
summary: Surveys retriever-centric, generator-centric, hybrid, and robustness-oriented RAG architectures. It highlights open challenges around adaptive retrieval, grounding, efficiency, and privacy-preserving retrieval.

title: MA-RAG: Multi-Agent Retrieval-Augmented Generation via Collaborative Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
topics: multi-agent RAG; planner; extractor; QA agent; ambiguous QA; multi-hop
summary: Orchestrates planner, step definer, extractor, and QA agents to decompose ambiguous information needs. The method improves multi-hop and ambiguous QA without domain-specific fine-tuning.

title: Accelerating Adaptive Retrieval Augmented Generation via Instruction-Driven Representation Reduction of Retrieval Overlaps
topics: adaptive RAG; efficiency; cache reuse; overlapping retrieval; parallel generation
summary: Speeds up iterative adaptive RAG by avoiding repeated representation of overlapping retrieved content. Combines cache access, parallel generation, and instruction guidance while preserving output quality.

title: Enhancing Cache-Augmented Generation (CAG) with Adaptive Contextual Compression for Scalable Knowledge Integration
topics: CAG; adaptive contextual compression; hybrid CAG-RAG; scalability; multi-hop
summary: Extends CAG with adaptive contextual compression and selective retrieval. The paper positions hybrid CAG-RAG as a practical way to reduce latency while handling knowledge that is too large or dynamic for pure caching.

title: HyperRAG: Enhancing Quality-Efficiency Tradeoffs in Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Reranker KV-Cache Reuse
topics: RAG efficiency; reranking; KV-cache reuse; throughput; latency
summary: Optimizes RAG service throughput by reusing document-side KV cache in decoder-only rerankers. It targets the quality gains of reranking without paying the full inference cost each time.

title: CoRAG: Collaborative Retrieval-Augmented Generation
topics: collaborative RAG; shared passage store; few-shot learning; federated knowledge
summary: Extends RAG into collaborative settings where clients benefit from a shared passage store. It improves low-resource QA but introduces risks from harmful or irrelevant shared passages.

title: Self-Routing RAG: Binding Selective Retrieval with Knowledge Verbalization
topics: selective retrieval; source routing; parametric knowledge; latency; calibration
summary: Treats the LLM itself as one selectable knowledge source alongside external stores. It reduces unnecessary retrievals by routing queries through internal knowledge, verbalized knowledge, or external data sources.

title: Beyond RAG: Task-Aware KV Cache Compression for Comprehensive Knowledge Reasoning
topics: KV cache compression; long context; CAG; broad knowledge reasoning; latency
summary: Compresses external knowledge into task-aware KV cache representations for efficient reasoning. It is positioned as better than top-k RAG when useful evidence is broad rather than sparse.

title: APE: Faster and Longer Context-Augmented Generation via Adaptive Parallel Encoding
topics: context-augmented generation; parallel encoding; KV cache; long context; speedup
summary: Pre-computes and loads KV states for many contexts in parallel. Adaptive alignment reduces the quality loss normally caused by parallel context encoding.

title: GFM-RAG: Graph Foundation Model for Retrieval Augmented Generation
topics: GraphRAG; graph foundation model; GNN; knowledge graphs; zero-shot retrieval
summary: Uses a graph foundation model trained over large graph and document corpora to reason over query-knowledge relationships. It aims to generalize graph-enhanced retrieval to unseen datasets without fine-tuning.

title: Chain-of-Retrieval Augmented Generation
topics: iterative retrieval; chain-of-retrieval; test-time scaling; query reformulation; multi-hop
summary: Trains models to retrieve and reason step by step before answering. It uses generated intermediate retrieval chains and test-time decoding strategies to improve multi-hop QA.

title: Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Survey on Agentic RAG
topics: agentic RAG survey; planning; reflection; tool use; multi-agent collaboration
summary: Surveys agentic RAG designs where agents manage retrieval strategy, planning, reflection, and tool use. It provides taxonomy and practical implementation challenges.

title: Don't Do RAG: When Cache-Augmented Generation is All You Need for Knowledge Tasks
topics: CAG; long-context LLM; retrieval-free QA; cached context; latency
summary: Argues that for constrained knowledge bases, preloading and caching relevant resources can remove retrieval latency and retrieval errors. It frames CAG as a simpler complement or alternative to RAG.

title: LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation
topics: LightRAG; graph indexing; dual-level retrieval; vector-graph retrieval; incremental update
summary: Combines graph structures with vector retrieval to capture low-level details and high-level relationships. It emphasizes fast, simple retrieval with incremental updates for changing data.

title: RAGChecker: A Fine-grained Framework for Diagnosing Retrieval-Augmented Generation
topics: RAG evaluation; diagnostics; retrieval metrics; generation metrics; system comparison
summary: Provides diagnostic metrics for both retrieval and generation modules. It helps identify whether failures come from missing evidence, poor selection, or unsupported generation.

title: G-Retriever: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Textual Graph Understanding and Question Answering
topics: textual graph QA; GNN; subgraph retrieval; PCST; hallucination reduction
summary: Enables question answering over textual graphs by retrieving relevant subgraphs. It formulates graph retrieval as a Prize-Collecting Steiner Tree problem and reduces hallucination on graph tasks.
